meta.title: Inside Our Music-CRS Recommender
meta.evidencePolicy: Outcome quality is labelled only from devset examples with visible ground truth. Blind-B is used to illustrate submitted mechanics because its relevance labels are hidden.

claims.claim: The Blind-B submission extracts a typed conversation state with DeepSeek V4 Flash, resolves entities and constraints, retrieves up to 1,000 candidates per branch, and applies the goal-free LightGBM bundle before returning 20 tracks.
claims.sourceNote: The active config specifies prompt_version v1, pool_k 500, top_k_out 1000, retrieval_topk 20, and models/reranker_v12_goalfree; the saved trace records extracted_state, resolver, retrieval, ranking, and final_recommendation stages.

claims.claim: Candidate generation combines lexical BM25, metadata and attribute text embeddings, lyric embeddings when routed, CLAP audio-text retrieval, SigLIP2 cover-image retrieval when routed, anchor/user centroids, artist discography, and era-popularity lookup.
claims.sourceNote: The config enumerates the active dense and centroid branches; the primary trace shows which branches fired or were skipped and why.

claims.claim: Exact track-and-artist requests are a demonstrated strength of the labelled devset snapshot: the ten exact-entity positive controls all landed in the final top two, including Numb at rank 1.
claims.sourceNote: Pack POS_exact_entity_success_control contains ten labelled positive controls with final ranks 1 or 2. This is evidence for the analysed devset baseline, not a Blind-B relevance claim.

claims.claim: The labelled devset baseline had substantial stage-localized headroom: final top-20 hit rate was 27.36%, while the union of branch top-20 lists contained the target on 47.66% of turns and the union of branch top-1000 lists on 90.54%.
claims.sourceNote: The frozen 2026-06-06 analysis reports final_hit@20=0.2736, union_hit@20=0.4766, and union_hit@1000=0.9054 over 8,000 devset turns. It predates the final submitted ranker and is used as diagnostic evidence, not an estimate of Blind-B quality.

claims.claim: The submitted response generator uses Qwen3-30B-A3B with latest-state conditioning and an XML item block capped at ten tags; the earlier bake-off found this model and state conditioning best among tested settings.
claims.sourceNote: The active config sets phase2_best_qwen, conditioning latest_state, item_format xml, max_tags 10, and temperature 0.0. The bake-off reports a 4.2/5 Blind-A judge score for Qwen3-30B-A3B with state conditioning; that score is not a Blind-B measurement.

claims.claim: The saved Blind-B trace proves execution mechanics but cannot establish that its recommendations are relevant.
claims.sourceNote: Blind-B ground-truth relevance is hidden. The analysis snapshot explicitly distinguishes blind-set metadata availability, so no Blind-B trace is labelled good or bad here.

primaryTrace.scope: A labelled frozen-baseline success establishes retrieval outcome quality; a separate Blind-B submitted-system trace establishes final mechanics and an observed completion-handling failure. They are not presented as one aligned run.

alignedGood.systemScope: Frozen 2026-06-06 labelled devset baseline, not the final submitted V1/LGBM system
conversation: Turn 1 requested energetic indie rock for long drives; Let's Dance to Joy Division by The Wombats was played.
Turn 2 asked for something else: a popular track with a memorable whistle or strong bassline, perhaps by Foster The People or Cage The Elephant.

state.turnIntent: I'm looking for an energetic indie rock track, popular, with a memorable whistle or a really strong bassline, maybe by a band like Foster The People or Cage The Elephant.

candidateEvidence.branchQueriesAndCounts: Not retained in the compact positive-control record

ranking.labelledTarget: Pumped Up Kicks — Foster The People
finalTopFive: (If You're Wondering If I Want You To) I Want You To

responseProvenance.retrievalHandoff: The labelled target Pumped Up Kicks was rank 1, so it aligns with a top-1 response handoff.
responseProvenance.datasetReferenceAssistant: The dataset row contains assistant prose recommending Pumped Up Kicks and citing popularity, whistle, and bassline, but this is reference conversation text, not a stored response generated from the frozen ranking run.

alignedGood.reconstructionStatus: Conversation, state summary, branch rank, fusion rank, final ranks, and dataset assistant text are recorded. The vertical stage layout is reconstructed; exact branch query payloads, branch counts, and generated handoff messages are unavailable.

submittedFailure.systemScope: Final Blind-B submitted V1 extractor and goal-free LightGBM bundle; relevance labels remain hidden
submittedFailure.conversation: After seven search turns, the listener explicitly confirms Pumped Up Kicks and says the search is complete.
submittedFailure.state: The saved state remains hidden_target/open_explore with six older facets and the identified track as positive satisfied feedback.

retrieval.centroidExplanation: Pumped Up Kicks enters all three compiler centroids through positive satisfied track_feedback; Sit Next to Me and Houdini are representative Foster The People tracks added by similar-artist anchoring.
retrieval.discographyExplanation: The discography query has no targets because it accepts exact_target artists; Foster The People was resolved but was ineligible under the satisfied-prior/style-reference role policy.

ranking.artifactMismatch: Trace and saved prediction agree through rank 14 and at ranks 17-19, but swap ranks 15/16 and contain different rank-20 IDs. Delivered top-20 claims use the saved prediction artifact.

submittedFailure.responseProvenance: Listener-facing Tongue Tied prose is verbatim from the saved prediction. Formatter messages are reconstructed from the trace, dataset row, catalog, and submitted code because outbound messages were not persisted.
submittedFailure.reconstructionStatus: Conversation, state, branch status/counts, ranking IDs, and saved response are recorded. Catalog labels and formatted response-model inputs are reconstructed; Blind-B relevance and causal impact remain unknown.

primaryTrace.sourceNote: Good spine: POS_clean_final_hit_control sample a61b366c-8cf5-48ad-a13f-181c033b9d89::t2. Submitted failure spine: Blind-B trace and prediction record 71 (zero-based 70), cross-checked against compiler role gates.

goodExamples.request: I'm looking for the original 2003 release of 'Numb' by Linkin Park from the 'Meteora' album.
goodExamples.whyStrong: The target was ranked first from an exact title, artist, album, and year request.
goodExamples.sourceNote: Labelled devset positive control POS_exact_entity_success_control; sample 0681d55b-98a0-4773-a9df-075a8050d805::t1.

goodExamples.request: More Gorillaz tracks with a strong electronic or synth vibe, regardless of whether they have a feature.
goodExamples.whyStrong: The system retained the artist while refining toward electronic and synth attributes, placing the labelled target fourth.
goodExamples.sourceNote: Labelled devset positive control POS_clean_final_hit_control; sample 737a65cf-9c45-4b1d-910d-2f1f1ef5eab7::t3.

goodExamples.request: I'm looking for an energetic indie rock track, popular, with a memorable whistle or a really strong bassline, maybe by a band like Foster The People or Cage The Elephant.
goodExamples.labelledTarget: Pumped Up Kicks — Foster The People
goodExamples.whyStrong: The listener pivoted away from the prior recommendation and explicitly suggested new artists, including Foster The People; the labelled target ranked first.
goodExamples.sourceNote: Labelled devset positive control POS_clean_final_hit_control; the extracted turn intent preserves Foster The People and Cage The Elephant, cohort labels include midconv_new_artist and novelty_cue, and BM25 was the best branch at rank 1.

goodExamples.request: Play the classic Ice Cube song from the 90s that starts with 'Just wakin' up in the morning, gotta thank God'.
goodExamples.labelledTarget: It Was A Good Day — Ice Cube
goodExamples.whyStrong: The request combined an artist, an era, and a quoted lyric clue; lyric_search routing fired and the labelled target ranked second. The best branch was resolved artist discography at rank 1, so this example does not establish that lyric retrieval caused the placement.
goodExamples.sourceNote: Labelled devset positive control POS_clean_final_hit_control; sample a9b423bf-d05c-418d-98af-2a3b1e1d7917::t1 records routing exact_entity_probe plus lyric_search, best branch lookup.resolved_artist_discography at rank 1, and final rank 2.

goodExamples.request: There's this Post Malone song I really like, but I can't quite describe what it is about it that hooks me.
goodExamples.whyStrong: Despite a deliberately underspecified memory query, the labelled target remained inside the returned top 20.
goodExamples.sourceNote: Labelled devset positive control POS_clean_final_hit_control; sample 13552d56-f3d8-443a-9272-11ec16c80fa1::t1.

failureExamples.request: Yes! 'Can You Feel the Force' is awesome. What are some other high-energy, classic disco or funk tracks from that late 70s to early 80s period?
failureExamples.labelledTarget: Give It To Me Baby — Rick James

observedPath.state: The prior artist The Real Thing remained a positive anchor even though the request asked for other tracks and the ideal state marks it satisfied/history.

failureExamples.firstBrokenBoundary: State extraction failed to type the prior artist as satisfied/history before candidate generation; the target then missed union@100 and could not be repaired by final ranking.
failureExamples.sourceNote: Labelled devset failure P0_roleless_stale_entity_failure; the pack includes recent messages, extracted state, ideal state, branch ranks, fused rank, and final rank.

failureExamples.request: An electronic band with a powerful, anthemic sound.
failureExamples.firstBrokenBoundary: No retrieval branch placed the labelled target in its top 1,000, so fusion and reranking had no opportunity to recover it.
failureExamples.sourceNote: Labelled devset example in not_in_any_branch_top1000; the target has no branch, fused, or final rank.

failureExamples.request: 80s American hardcore punk bands known for their raw energy and short, intense songs.
failureExamples.firstBrokenBoundary: A strong branch retrieved the target at rank 6, but fusion demoted it outside the top 20 before the final ranker moved it farther down.
failureExamples.sourceNote: Labelled devset example in union20_fusion_loss / branch_top20_fusion_miss.

failureExamples.request: More 80s American hardcore punk bands that incorporated a bit more melody or even some metallic influences into their sound, while still keeping that raw energy.
failureExamples.labelledTarget: Too Drunk to Fuck — Dead Kennedys
failureExamples.firstBrokenBoundary: The target survived retrieval and fusion inside the top 20, but final ranking demoted it to rank 74.
failureExamples.sourceNote: Labelled devset example in union20_postfusion_or_final_loss / fused_top20_postfusion_demoted.

gaps.expectedCapability: Measure whether the final Blind-B top-20 lists and generated replies satisfy listeners.
gaps.submittedBehavior: The trace records mechanics and outputs, but Blind-B relevance labels and judge scores are hidden.
gaps.evidence: The Blind-B JSONL contains no ground-truth target or relevance field; the analysis snapshot documents blind-set metadata availability separately from labelled devset outcomes.
gaps.userConsequence: We cannot honestly classify an individual submitted trace as a recommendation success or failure from this artifact alone.
gaps.recoverability: Recoverable only when organizer evaluation or labels become available; until then use labelled devset traces for outcome claims.

gaps.expectedCapability: Place the relevant track into at least one candidate branch deeply enough for fusion and reranking to act.
gaps.submittedBehavior: In the frozen devset baseline, 757 of 8,000 turns (9.46%) had the target absent from every branch top 1,000.
gaps.evidence: recall_gap_data.json reports not_in_any_branch_top1000=757 and includes the VNV Nation hidden-target example with no branch rank.
gaps.userConsequence: Once the target is absent from all candidate pools, no downstream ranker can recommend it.
gaps.recoverability: Requires better state-to-query translation, new or improved retrieval branches, deeper pools, or catalog/index improvements; reranking alone cannot recover these cases.

gaps.expectedCapability: Preserve candidates that a specialist branch ranks highly when combining heterogeneous retrievers.
gaps.submittedBehavior: The frozen baseline lost 1,046 targets (13.08% of turns) that appeared in a branch top 20 but not in union/fused top 20.
gaps.evidence: recall_gap_data.json reports union20_fusion_loss=1,046; Hard Times was rank 6 in metadata retrieval but rank 60 after fusion.
gaps.userConsequence: A correct specialist signal can be diluted by other branches before the learned final ranker sees a practical top list.
gaps.recoverability: Replay fusion with calibrated branch weights or learned fusion features, then verify that gains do not regress exact-entity controls.

gaps.expectedCapability: Keep relevant candidates in the final top 20 after they survive fusion.
gaps.submittedBehavior: The baseline has examples where a target was top 20 after fusion but was demoted by final ranking.
gaps.evidence: Too Drunk to Fuck was branch rank 4 and fused rank 13, then final rank 74 in the labelled devset snapshot.
gaps.userConsequence: The system retrieves the right item but does not expose it in the delivered recommendation list.
gaps.recoverability: Improve ranker features and training labels, then replay the saved candidate trace to isolate ranker changes from retrieval changes.

gaps.expectedCapability: Distinguish current targets from accepted, satisfied, rejected, or merely historical entities across turns.
gaps.submittedBehavior: The frozen 2026-06-06 roleless devset baseline sometimes retained a prior artist as a positive retrieval anchor after the listener asked for other artists or directions. The submitted config uses the newer V1 extractor, so this snapshot does not prove that the submitted system retains the same failure.
gaps.evidence: P0_roleless_stale_entity_failure identifies stale/satisfied entities acting as current anchors in the frozen baseline, including The Real Thing on the Rick James target turn; configs/state_ranker_v10_lgbm_blindset_B.yaml separately specifies prompt_version v1 for the submission.
gaps.userConsequence: In the observed baseline cases, retrieval remained anchored to a prior answer instead of fully following the listener's refinement or pivot; impact on the submitted V1 path is unknown from this evidence.
gaps.recoverability: Re-evaluate the same labelled stale-entity cohort with V1 state traces. If failures persist, tighten source-turn, recency, entity-role, and seed-use semantics while using continuation controls to guard regressions.

gaps.expectedCapability: Generate concise, personalized explanations that generalize across the full multilingual challenge distribution.
gaps.submittedBehavior: The chosen Qwen/state-conditioned setup was selected from an eight-session, English-only proxy slice and validated on Blind-A, then enabled in the Blind-B config.
gaps.evidence: The bake-off reports the 4.2 Blind-A result but explicitly calls for a larger, non-English evaluation slice and notes the proxy judge is only a coarse ranker.
gaps.userConsequence: Response quality may vary for languages, conversational styles, or failure modes not represented in the small bake-off slice.
gaps.recoverability: Evaluate a broader labelled multilingual replay set and compare response quality separately from retrieval quality.

sources.role: Active submitted configuration and exact model, routing, retrieval, ranking, and response-generation settings.
Saved submitted-system mechanics; illustrative only for outcomes because Blind-B labels are hidden.
Labelled devset positive controls, full state failures, recent conversation context, and ideal-state targets.
Frozen ranker diagnostics, examples, caveats, metrics, and stage-localized decisions.
Frozen 8,000-turn devset recall ceilings, failure buckets, branch ranks, fusion ranks, and final ranks.
Point-in-time verified-bug audit and resolution status; used to avoid presenting resolved or retracted findings as current behavior.
Response-generation experiment design, proxy caveats, Blind-A results, chosen model, and conditioning decision.
